34: @Injectable()
35: export class DataQualityService {
36:   private readonly logger = new Logger(DataQualityService.name);
37:   private lastReport: DataQualityReport | null = null;
38: 
39:   constructor(
40:     @InjectRepository(DuplicateCandidate)
41:     private readonly dupRepo: Repository<DuplicateCandidate>,
42:     @InjectRepository(EntityMapping)
43:     private readonly mappingRepo: Repository<EntityMapping>,
44:     private readonly dataSource: DataSource,
45:   ) {}
46: 
47:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Fingerprint ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
48: 
49:   generateFingerprint(parts: (string | null | undefined)[]): string {
50:     return parts
51:       .filter((p): p is string => typeof p === 'string' && p.length > 0)
52:       .map((p) =>
53:         p
54:           .toLowerCase()
55:           .trim()
56:           .replace(/[^a-z0-9\s]/g, ' ')
57:           .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
58:           .trim(),
59:       )
60:       .join('|');
61:   }
62: 
63:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Duplicate detection ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
64: 
65:   /**
66:    * Checks for duplicates for a single entity.
67:    * When called inside a transaction (e.g., from runDedupScan), pass the
68:    * EntityManager so writes participate in the transaction and are rolled back
69:    * atomically on failure.
70:    */
71:   async checkForDuplicates(
72:     entityType: 'Supplier' | 'Article' | 'CatalogItem',
73:     id: string,
74:     fingerprint: string,
75:     manager?: EntityManager,
76:   ): Promise<void> {
77:     if (!fingerprint) return;
78: 
79:     const tableMap: Record<string, string> = {
80:       Supplier: 'suppliers',
81:       Article: 'articles',
82:       CatalogItem: 'catalog_items',
83:     };
84:     const table = tableMap[entityType];
85:     const db = manager ?? this.dataSource;
86:     const dupRepo = manager
87:       ? manager.getRepository(DuplicateCandidate)
88:       : this.dupRepo;
89: 
90:     const rows: Array<{ id: string; score: string }> = await db.query(
91:       `
92:       SELECT id, similarity(fingerprint, $1) AS score
93:       FROM ${table}
94:       WHERE id != $2
95:         AND fingerprint IS NOT NULL
96:         AND similarity(fingerprint, $1) >= $3
97:       ORDER BY score DESC
98:       LIMIT 10
99:       `,
100:       [fingerprint, id, FUZZY_THRESHOLD],
101:     );
102: 
103:     for (const row of rows) {
104:       const score = parseFloat(row.score);
105:       // Canonical pair ordering (smaller UUID first) prevents duplicate pairs
106:       const [sourceId, targetId] = id < row.id ? [id, row.id] : [row.id, id];
107: 
108:       const existing = await dupRepo.findOne({
109:         where: { entityType, sourceId, targetId },
110:       });
111: 
112:       if (existing) {
113:         // Update score if improved
114:         if (score > Number(existing.similarityScore)) {
115:           await dupRepo.update(existing.id, {
116:             similarityScore: score,
117:             isAutoMergeCandidate: score >= AUTO_MERGE_THRESHOLD,
118:             status:
119:               existing.status === DuplicateCandidateStatus.DISMISSED
120:                 ? DuplicateCandidateStatus.DISMISSED
121:                 : DuplicateCandidateStatus.PENDING_REVIEW,
122:           });
123:         }
124:         continue;
125:       }
126: 
127:       await dupRepo.save({
128:         entityType,
129:         sourceId,
130:         targetId,
131:         similarityScore: score,
132:         isAutoMergeCandidate: score >= AUTO_MERGE_THRESHOLD,
133:         status: DuplicateCandidateStatus.PENDING_REVIEW,
134:       });
135: 
136:       this.logger.log(
137:         `Duplicate candidate: ${entityType} ${sourceId} Γåö ${targetId} (${(score * 100).toFixed(1)}%)`,
138:       );
139:     }
140:   }
141: 
142:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Merge ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
143: 
144:   async mergeDuplicate(candidateId: string, adminId: string): Promise<void> {
145:     const candidate = await this.dupRepo.findOne({ where: { id: candidateId } });
146:     if (!candidate) throw new NotFoundException('Duplicate candidate not found');
147:     if (candidate.status !== DuplicateCandidateStatus.PENDING_REVIEW) {
148:       throw new BadRequestException(`Candidate is already ${candidate.status}`);
149:     }
150: 
151:     // sourceId = primary (kept), targetId = secondary (soft-deleted)
152:     const primaryId = candidate.sourceId;
153:     const secondaryId = candidate.targetId;
154: 
155:     await this.dataSource.transaction(async (manager) => {
156:       if (candidate.entityType === 'Supplier') {
157:         await this.mergeSuppliers(manager, primaryId, secondaryId);
158:       } else if (candidate.entityType === 'Article') {
159:         await this.mergeArticles(manager, primaryId, secondaryId);
160:       } else if (candidate.entityType === 'CatalogItem') {
161:         await this.mergeCatalogItems(manager, primaryId, secondaryId);
162:       }
163: 
164:       // Record the mapping
165:       await manager.save(EntityMapping, {
166:         entityType: candidate.entityType,
167:         oldId: secondaryId,
168:         newId: primaryId,
169:         mergedBy: adminId,
170:       });
171: 
172:       // Mark candidate as merged
173:       await manager.update(DuplicateCandidate, candidateId, {
174:         status: DuplicateCandidateStatus.MERGED,
175:         reviewedBy: adminId,
176:         reviewedAt: new Date(),
177:       });
178: 
179:       // Dismiss other candidates that involve the secondary record
180:       await manager
181:         .createQueryBuilder()
182:         .update(DuplicateCandidate)
183:         .set({
184:           status: DuplicateCandidateStatus.DISMISSED,
185:           reviewedBy: adminId,
186:           reviewedAt: new Date(),
187:         })
188:         .where(
189:           '("sourceId" = :sid OR "targetId" = :sid) AND status = :status AND id != :cid',
190:           {
191:             sid: secondaryId,
192:             status: DuplicateCandidateStatus.PENDING_REVIEW,
193:             cid: candidateId,
194:           },
195:         )
196:         .execute();
197:     });
198:   }
199: 
200:   private async mergeSuppliers(
201:     manager: EntityManager,
202:     primaryId: string,
203:     secondaryId: string,
204:   ): Promise<void> {
205:     // Rewire FKs to primary
206:     await manager.query(
207:       `UPDATE purchase_requests SET "supplierId" = $1 WHERE "supplierId" = $2`,
208:       [primaryId, secondaryId],
209:     );
210:     await manager.query(
211:       `UPDATE purchase_orders SET "supplierId" = $1 WHERE "supplierId" = $2`,
212:       [primaryId, secondaryId],
213:     );
214:     await manager.query(
215:       `UPDATE return_authorizations SET "supplierId" = $1 WHERE "supplierId" = $2`,
216:       [primaryId, secondaryId],
217:     );
218:     await manager.query(
219:       `UPDATE funds_ledger_entries SET "supplierId" = $1 WHERE "supplierId" = $2`,
220:       [primaryId, secondaryId],
221:     );
222:     // Soft-delete secondary
223:     await manager.query(`UPDATE suppliers SET "isActive" = false WHERE id = $1`, [secondaryId]);
224:   }
225: 
226:   private async mergeArticles(
227:     manager: EntityManager,
228:     primaryId: string,
229:     secondaryId: string,
230:   ): Promise<void> {
231:     // Rewire FKs to primary
232:     await manager.query(
233:       `UPDATE article_versions SET "articleId" = $1 WHERE "articleId" = $2`,
234:       [primaryId, secondaryId],
235:     );
236:     // Upsert favorites (avoid PK conflict: skip if user already favorited primary)
237:     await manager.query(
238:       `INSERT INTO user_favorites ("userId", "articleId", "createdAt")
239:        SELECT "userId", $1, "createdAt"
240:        FROM user_favorites
241:        WHERE "articleId" = $2
242:          AND "userId" NOT IN (SELECT "userId" FROM user_favorites WHERE "articleId" = $1)`,
243:       [primaryId, secondaryId],
244:     );
245:     await manager.query(`DELETE FROM user_favorites WHERE "articleId" = $1`, [secondaryId]);
246:     // Archive secondary
247:     await manager.query(`UPDATE articles SET status = 'ARCHIVED' WHERE id = $1`, [secondaryId]);
248:   }
249: 
250:   private async mergeCatalogItems(
251:     manager: EntityManager,
252:     primaryId: string,
253:     secondaryId: string,
254:   ): Promise<void> {
255:     // Rewire purchase-order and purchase-request line items that reference the catalog item
256:     await manager.query(
257:       `UPDATE purchase_order_line_items SET "catalogItemId" = $1 WHERE "catalogItemId" = $2`,
258:       [primaryId, secondaryId],
259:     );
260:     await manager.query(
261:       `UPDATE purchase_request_line_items SET "catalogItemId" = $1 WHERE "catalogItemId" = $2`,
262:       [primaryId, secondaryId],
263:     );
264:     // Deactivate secondary
265:     await manager.query(`UPDATE catalog_items SET "isActive" = false WHERE id = $1`, [secondaryId]);
266:   }
267: 
268:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Dismiss ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
269: 
270:   async dismissDuplicate(candidateId: string, adminId: string): Promise<void> {
271:     const candidate = await this.dupRepo.findOne({ where: { id: candidateId } });
272:     if (!candidate) throw new NotFoundException('Duplicate candidate not found');
273:     if (candidate.status !== DuplicateCandidateStatus.PENDING_REVIEW) {
274:       throw new BadRequestException(`Candidate is already ${candidate.status}`);
275:     }
276:     await this.dupRepo.update(candidateId, {
277:       status: DuplicateCandidateStatus.DISMISSED,
278:       reviewedBy: adminId,
279:       reviewedAt: new Date(),
280:     });
281:   }
282: 
283:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Query duplicates ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
284: 
285:   async getDuplicates(
286:     status?: string,
287:     entityType?: string,
288:   ): Promise<DuplicateCandidate[]> {
289:     const qb = this.dupRepo
290:       .createQueryBuilder('dc')
291:       .leftJoinAndSelect('dc.reviewer', 'reviewer')
292:       .orderBy('dc.similarityScore', 'DESC')
293:       .addOrderBy('dc.createdAt', 'DESC');
294: 
295:     if (status) qb.andWhere('dc.status = :status', { status });
296:     if (entityType) qb.andWhere('dc.entityType = :entityType', { entityType });
297: 
298:     return qb.getMany();
299:   }
300: 
301:   async getDuplicateWithDetails(id: string): Promise<{
302:     candidate: DuplicateCandidate;
303:     source: Record<string, unknown> | null;
304:     target: Record<string, unknown> | null;
305:   }> {
306:     const candidate = await this.dupRepo.findOne({ where: { id } });
307:     if (!candidate) throw new NotFoundException('Duplicate candidate not found');
308: 
309:     const tableMap: Record<string, string> = {
310:       Supplier: 'suppliers',
311:       Article: 'articles',
312:       CatalogItem: 'catalog_items',
313:     };
314:     const columnsMap: Record<string, string> = {
315:       Supplier: 'id, name, "contactName", email, phone, address, "paymentTerms", "isActive", fingerprint',
316:       Article: 'id, title, slug, category, status, tags, fingerprint, "updatedAt"',
317:       CatalogItem: 'id, title, "supplierId", "unitSize", upc, "unitPrice", "isActive", fingerprint',
318:     };
319:     const table = tableMap[candidate.entityType] ?? 'suppliers';
320:     const columns = columnsMap[candidate.entityType] ?? 'id, fingerprint';
321: 
322:     const [source] = await this.dataSource.query(
323:       `SELECT ${columns} FROM ${table} WHERE id = $1`,
324:       [candidate.sourceId],
325:     );
326:     const [target] = await this.dataSource.query(
327:       `SELECT ${columns} FROM ${table} WHERE id = $1`,
328:       [candidate.targetId],
329:     );
330: 
331:     return {
332:       candidate,
333:       source: source ?? null,
334:       target: target ?? null,
335:     };
336:   }
337: 
338:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Data quality checks ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
339: 
340:   async runQualityChecks(): Promise<DataQualityReport> {
341:     this.logger.log('Running data quality checksΓÇª');
342:     const issues: DataQualityIssue[] = [];
343: 
344:     // 1. Suppliers missing email
345:     const missingEmail: Array<{ id: string; name: string }> = await this.dataSource.query(
346:       `SELECT id, name FROM suppliers WHERE "isActive" = true AND (email IS NULL OR email = '')`,
347:     );
348:     for (const s of missingEmail) {
349:       issues.push({
350:         type: 'MISSING_SUPPLIER_EMAIL',
351:         entityType: 'Supplier',
352:         entityId: s.id,
353:         label: s.name,
354:         detail: 'Supplier is missing an email address.',
355:       });
356:     }
357: 
358:     // 2. Suppliers missing payment terms (null or default is fine; flag if customTermsDescription is non-null but paymentTerms is null)
359:     //    Actually per spec: "Missing required fields: email, payment terms"
360:     //    Since paymentTerms has a DB default, let's flag suppliers where paymentTerms is NULL
361:     const missingPaymentTerms: Array<{ id: string; name: string }> = await this.dataSource.query(
362:       `SELECT id, name FROM suppliers WHERE "isActive" = true AND "paymentTerms" IS NULL`,
363:     );
364:     for (const s of missingPaymentTerms) {
365:       issues.push({
366:         type: 'MISSING_PAYMENT_TERMS',
367:         entityType: 'Supplier',
368:         entityId: s.id,
369:         label: s.name,
370:         detail: 'Supplier is missing payment terms.',
371:       });
372:     }
373: 
374:     // 3. Duplicate suppliers by name similarity (not already flagged by fingerprint dedup)
375:     const dupSuppliers: Array<{ id1: string; name1: string; id2: string; name2: string; score: string }> =
376:       await this.dataSource.query(`
377:         SELECT
378:           s1.id AS id1, s1.name AS name1,
379:           s2.id AS id2, s2.name AS name2,
380:           similarity(s1.name, s2.name) AS score
381:         FROM suppliers s1
382:         JOIN suppliers s2 ON s1.id < s2.id
383:         WHERE s1."isActive" = true AND s2."isActive" = true
384:           AND similarity(s1.name, s2.name) >= 0.80
385:         ORDER BY score DESC
386:         LIMIT 20
387:       `);
388:     for (const d of dupSuppliers) {
389:       issues.push({
390:         type: 'DUPLICATE_SUPPLIER',
391:         entityType: 'Supplier',
392:         entityId: d.id1,
393:         label: `${d.name1} / ${d.name2}`,
394:         detail: `Name similarity: ${(parseFloat(d.score) * 100).toFixed(1)}%. Possible duplicate with supplier ${d.id2}.`,
395:       });
396:     }
397: 
398:     // 4. Outlier pricing: PO line items with unit price > 3├ù median for the same description
399:     const outliers: Array<{ id: string; description: string; unitPrice: string; median: string; poId: string }> =
400:       await this.dataSource.query(`
401:         WITH medians AS (
402:           SELECT
403:             description,
404:             PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "unitPrice") AS median_price
405:           FROM purchase_order_line_items
406:           GROUP BY description
407:           HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
408:         )
409:         SELECT li.id, li.description, li."unitPrice", li."poId", m.median_price AS median
410:         FROM purchase_order_line_items li
411:         JOIN medians m ON m.description = li.description
412:         WHERE li."unitPrice" > m.median_price * 3
413:         ORDER BY (li."unitPrice" / m.median_price) DESC
414:         LIMIT 50
415:       `);
416:     for (const o of outliers) {
417:       issues.push({
418:         type: 'OUTLIER_PRICING',
419:         entityType: 'PurchaseOrderLineItem',
420:         entityId: o.id,
421:         label: o.description,
422:         detail: `Unit price $${parseFloat(o.unitPrice).toFixed(2)} is ${(parseFloat(o.unitPrice) / parseFloat(o.median)).toFixed(1)}├ù the median ($${parseFloat(o.median).toFixed(2)}) on PO ${o.poId}.`,
423:       });
424:     }
425: 
426:     const report: DataQualityReport = {
427:       checkedAt: new Date(),
428:       issues,
429:       counts: {
430:         missingEmail: missingEmail.length,
431:         missingPaymentTerms: missingPaymentTerms.length,
432:         duplicateSuppliers: dupSuppliers.length,
433:         outlierPricing: outliers.length,
434:       },
435:     };
436: 
437:     this.lastReport = report;
438:     this.logger.log(`Data quality check complete: ${issues.length} issue(s) found.`);
439:     return report;
440:   }
441: 
442:   async getLastQualityReport(): Promise<DataQualityReport | null> {
443:     return this.lastReport;
444:   }
445: 
446:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Full dedup scan ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
447: 
448:   /**
449:    * Runs a full dedup scan wrapped in a single database transaction.
450:    * If any write fails, the entire scan for this attempt is rolled back,
451:    * preventing partial writes that could corrupt duplicate-candidate state
452:    * across retry attempts.
453:    */
454:   async runDedupScan(): Promise<void> {
455:     this.logger.log('Running full dedup scanΓÇª');
456: 
457:     let supplierCount = 0;
458:     let articleCount = 0;
459:     let catalogCount = 0;
460: 
461:     await this.dataSource.transaction(async (manager) => {
462:       // Scan suppliers that have a fingerprint
463:       const suppliers: Array<{ id: string; fingerprint: string }> = await manager.query(
464:         `SELECT id, fingerprint FROM suppliers WHERE fingerprint IS NOT NULL AND "isActive" = true`,
465:       );
466:       supplierCount = suppliers.length;
467:       for (const s of suppliers) {
468:         await this.checkForDuplicates('Supplier', s.id, s.fingerprint, manager);
469:       }
470: 
471:       // Scan articles that have a fingerprint
472:       const articles: Array<{ id: string; fingerprint: string }> = await manager.query(
473:         `SELECT id, fingerprint FROM articles WHERE fingerprint IS NOT NULL AND status != 'ARCHIVED'`,
474:       );
475:       articleCount = articles.length;
476:       for (const a of articles) {
477:         await this.checkForDuplicates('Article', a.id, a.fingerprint, manager);
478:       }
479: 
480:       // Scan catalog items that have a fingerprint
481:       const catalogItems: Array<{ id: string; fingerprint: string }> = await manager.query(
482:         `SELECT id, fingerprint FROM catalog_items WHERE fingerprint IS NOT NULL AND "isActive" = true`,
483:       );
484:       catalogCount = catalogItems.length;
485:       for (const c of catalogItems) {
486:         await this.checkForDuplicates('CatalogItem', c.id, c.fingerprint, manager);
487:       }
488:     });
489: 
490:     this.logger.log(
491:       `Dedup scan complete: checked ${supplierCount} suppliers, ${articleCount} articles, ${catalogCount} catalog items.`,
492:     );
493:   }
494: 
495:   async getPendingCount(): Promise<number> {
496:     return this.dupRepo.count({ where: { status: DuplicateCandidateStatus.PENDING_REVIEW } });
497:   }
498: }

<content>1: import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
2: import { InjectRepository } from '@nestjs/typeorm';
3: import { Repository, DataSource, MoreThanOrEqual, LessThanOrEqual, Between } from 'typeorm';
4: import { SystemLog } from './entities/system-log.entity';
5: import { JobRun } from './entities/job-run.entity';
6: 
7: export interface WriteLogInput {
8:   requestId?: string | null;
9:   userId?: string | null;
10:   level?: string;
11:   service?: string | null;
12:   message: string;
13:   method?: string | null;
14:   path?: string | null;
15:   statusCode?: number | null;
16:   durationMs?: number | null;
17:   metadata?: Record<string, unknown> | null;
18: }
19: 
20: // Cron schedule descriptions for display
21: const JOB_SCHEDULES: Record<string, string> = {
22:   'dedup-scan': 'Every 6 hours',
23:   'data-quality-check': 'Every 6 hours',
24:   'notification-queue-drain': 'Every hour',
25:   'session-cleanup': 'Every hour',
26: };
27: 
28: @Injectable()
29: export class ObservabilityService {
30:   constructor(
31:     @InjectRepository(SystemLog)
32:     private readonly logRepo: Repository<SystemLog>,
33:     @InjectRepository(JobRun)
34:     private readonly jobRunRepo: Repository<JobRun>,
35:     private readonly dataSource: DataSource,
36:   ) {}
37: 
38:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Structured logging ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
39: 
40:   async writeLog(input: WriteLogInput): Promise<void> {
41:     try {
42:       await this.logRepo.save({
43:         requestId: input.requestId ?? null,
44:         userId: input.userId ?? null,
45:         level: input.level ?? 'INFO',
46:         service: input.service ?? null,
47:         message: input.message,
48:         method: input.method ?? null,
49:         path: input.path ?? null,
50:         statusCode: input.statusCode ?? null,
51:         durationMs: input.durationMs ?? null,
52:         metadata: input.metadata ?? null,
53:       });
54:     } catch {
55:       // Never let logging errors bubble up and break the request
56:     }
57:   }
58: 
59:   async queryLogs(filters: {
60:     level?: string;
61:     service?: string;
62:     from?: string;
63:     to?: string;
64:     page?: number;
65:     limit?: number;
66:   }) {
67:     const page = filters.page ?? 1;
68:     const limit = Math.min(filters.limit ?? 50, 200);
69:     const skip = (page - 1) * limit;
70: 
71:     const qb = this.logRepo
72:       .createQueryBuilder('l')
73:       .orderBy('l.createdAt', 'DESC')
74:       .skip(skip)
75:       .take(limit);
76: 
77:     if (filters.level) qb.andWhere('l.level = :level', { level: filters.level });
78:     if (filters.service) qb.andWhere('l.service = :service', { service: filters.service });
79:     if (filters.from && filters.to) {
80:       qb.andWhere('l.createdAt BETWEEN :from AND :to', {
81:         from: new Date(filters.from),
82:         to: new Date(filters.to),
83:       });
84:     } else if (filters.from) {
85:       qb.andWhere('l.createdAt >= :from', { from: new Date(filters.from) });
86:     } else if (filters.to) {
87:       qb.andWhere('l.createdAt <= :to', { to: new Date(filters.to) });
88:     }
89: 
90:     const [data, total] = await qb.getManyAndCount();
91:     return { data, meta: { page, limit, total, totalPages: Math.ceil(total / limit) } };
92:   }
93: 
94:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Job run tracking ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
95: 
96:   async startJobRun(jobName: string, attempt = 1): Promise<JobRun> {
97:     return this.jobRunRepo.save({
98:       jobName,
99:       status: 'RUNNING',
100:       attempt,
101:       startedAt: new Date(),
102:     });
103:   }
104: 
105:   async completeJobRun(id: string, durationMs: number): Promise<void> {
106:     await this.jobRunRepo.update(id, {
107:       status: 'SUCCESS',
108:       finishedAt: new Date(),
109:       durationMs,
110:     });
111:   }
112: 
113:   async failJobRun(id: string, durationMs: number, errorMessage: string): Promise<void> {
114:     await this.jobRunRepo.update(id, {
115:       status: 'FAILED',
116:       finishedAt: new Date(),
117:       durationMs,
118:       errorMessage,
119:     });
120:   }
121: 
122:   async getJobRun(id: string): Promise<JobRun | null> {
123:     return this.jobRunRepo.findOne({ where: { id } });
124:   }
125: 
126:   async getJobMetrics(): Promise<{
127:     jobs: Array<{
128:       jobName: string;
129:       schedule: string;
130:       lastRun: JobRun | null;
131:       successCount: number;
132:       failureCount: number;
133:     }>;
134:   }> {
135:     const jobNames = Object.keys(JOB_SCHEDULES);
136: 
137:     const jobs = await Promise.all(
138:       jobNames.map(async (jobName) => {
139:         const lastRun = await this.jobRunRepo.findOne({
140:           where: { jobName },
141:           order: { startedAt: 'DESC' },
142:         });
143:         const successCount = await this.jobRunRepo.count({ where: { jobName, status: 'SUCCESS' } });
144:         const failureCount = await this.jobRunRepo.count({ where: { jobName, status: 'FAILED' } });
145: 
146:         return {
147:           jobName,
148:           schedule: JOB_SCHEDULES[jobName] ?? 'ΓÇö',
149:           lastRun: lastRun ?? null,
150:           successCount,
151:           failureCount,
152:         };
153:       }),
154:     );
155: 
156:     return { jobs };
157:   }
158: 
159:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Queue stats ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
160: 
161:   async getQueueStats(): Promise<{
162:     pendingNotifications: number;
163:     pendingDuplicateCandidates: number;
164:   }> {
165:     const [notifResult, dupResult] = await Promise.all([
166:       this.dataSource.query<Array<{ count: string }>>(
167:         `SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM notifications WHERE "isQueued" = true`,
168:       ),
169:       this.dataSource.query<Array<{ count: string }>>(
170:         `SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM duplicate_candidates WHERE status = 'PENDING_REVIEW'`,
171:       ),
172:     ]);
173: 
174:     return {
175:       pendingNotifications: parseInt(notifResult[0]?.count ?? '0', 10),
176:       pendingDuplicateCandidates: parseInt(dupResult[0]?.count ?? '0', 10),
177:     };
178:   }
179: 
180:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ System stats ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
181: 
182:   async getSystemStats(): Promise<{
183:     dbConnections: { active: number; idle: number; total: number };
184:     tableSizes: Array<{ table: string; sizeBytes: number; prettySize: string }>;
185:     uptimeSeconds: number;
186:   }> {
187:     const [connRows, sizeRows, uptimeRows] = await Promise.all([
188:       this.dataSource.query<Array<{ state: string; count: string }>>(`
189:         SELECT state, COUNT(*) AS count
190:         FROM pg_stat_activity
191:         WHERE datname = current_database()
192:         GROUP BY state
193:       `),
194:       this.dataSource.query<Array<{ table: string; size: string; pretty: string }>>(`
195:         SELECT
196:           relname AS table,
197:           pg_total_relation_size(quote_ident(relname)) AS size,
198:           pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size(quote_ident(relname))) AS pretty
199:         FROM pg_stat_user_tables
200:         ORDER BY size DESC
201:         LIMIT 20
202:       `),
203:       this.dataSource.query<Array<{ uptime: string }>>(`
204:         SELECT EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - pg_postmaster_start_time()))::INTEGER AS uptime
205:       `),
206:     ]);
207: 
208:     const connMap = new Map(connRows.map((r) => [r.state, parseInt(r.count, 10)]));
209:     const active = connMap.get('active') ?? 0;
210:     const idle = connMap.get('idle') ?? 0;
211: 
212:     return {
213:       dbConnections: { active, idle, total: active + idle },
214:       tableSizes: sizeRows.map((r) => ({
215:         table: r.table,
216:         sizeBytes: parseInt(r.size, 10),
217:         prettySize: r.pretty,
218:       })),
219:       uptimeSeconds: parseInt(uptimeRows[0]?.uptime ?? '0', 10),
220:     };
221:   }
222: }

<content>1: import {
2:   Injectable,
3:   NestInterceptor,
4:   ExecutionContext,
5:   CallHandler,
6: } from '@nestjs/common';
7: import { Observable, tap, catchError, throwError } from 'rxjs';
8: import * as crypto from 'crypto';
9: import { ObservabilityService } from '../../observability/observability.service';
10: 
11: const SENSITIVE_KEYS = new Set([
12:   'password',
13:   'passwordHash',
14:   'token',
15:   'accessToken',
16:   'refreshToken',
17:   'bankingNotes',
18:   'internalRiskFlag',
19:   'secret',
20:   'authorization',
21: ]);
22: 
23: function redact(obj: unknown, depth = 0): unknown {
24:   if (depth > 4 || obj === null || obj === undefined) return obj;
25:   if (typeof obj !== 'object') return obj;
26:   if (Array.isArray(obj)) return obj.map((v) => redact(v, depth + 1));
27:   const out: Record<string, unknown> = {};
28:   for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(obj as Record<string, unknown>)) {
29:     out[k] = SENSITIVE_KEYS.has(k.toLowerCase()) || SENSITIVE_KEYS.has(k)
30:       ? '[REDACTED]'
31:       : redact(v, depth + 1);
32:   }
33:   return out;
34: }
35: 
36: @Injectable()
37: export class LoggingInterceptor implements NestInterceptor {
38:   constructor(private readonly observability: ObservabilityService) {}
39: 
40:   intercept(context: ExecutionContext, next: CallHandler): Observable<unknown> {
41:     const req = context.switchToHttp().getRequest<{
42:       method: string;
43:       url: string;
44:       user?: { id?: string };
45:       requestId?: string;
46:       body?: unknown;
47:     }>();
48: 
49:     const requestId = crypto.randomBytes(8).toString('hex');
50:     req.requestId = requestId;
51: 
52:     const start = Date.now();
53:     const method = req.method;
54:     const path = req.url;
55:     const userId = req.user?.id ?? null;
56: 
57:     // Skip logging for health checks and log endpoint itself to avoid noise
58:     const isNoise =
59:       path === '/api/health' ||
60:       path.startsWith('/api/admin/logs');
61: 
62:     return next.handle().pipe(
63:       tap(() => {
64:         if (isNoise) return;
65:         const res = context.switchToHttp().getResponse<{ statusCode: number }>();
66:         const statusCode = res.statusCode;
67:         const durationMs = Date.now() - start;
68:         void this.observability.writeLog({
69:           requestId,
70:           userId,
71:           level: statusCode >= 500 ? 'ERROR' : statusCode >= 400 ? 'WARN' : 'INFO',
72:           service: 'HTTP',
73:           message: `${method} ${path} ${statusCode}`,
74:           method,
75:           path,
76:           statusCode,
77:           durationMs,
78:         });
79:       }),
80:       catchError((err: unknown) => {
81:         if (!isNoise) {
82:           const statusCode =
83:             typeof err === 'object' && err !== null && 'status' in err
84:               ? (err as { status: number }).status
85:               : 500;
86:           const message =
87:             typeof err === 'object' && err !== null && 'message' in err
88:               ? String((err as { message: unknown }).message)
89:               : 'Internal error';
90:           const durationMs = Date.now() - start;
91:           void this.observability.writeLog({
92:             requestId,
93:             userId,
94:             level: 'ERROR',
95:             service: 'HTTP',
96:             message: `${method} ${path} ${statusCode} ΓÇö ${message}`,
97:             method,
98:             path,
99:             statusCode,
100:             durationMs,
101:           });
102:         }
103:         return throwError(() => err);
104:       }),
105:     );
106:   }
107: }

<content>1: import * as crypto from 'crypto';
2: 
3: const ALGORITHM = 'aes-256-gcm';
4: const IV_LENGTH = 16;
5: const AUTH_TAG_LENGTH = 16;
6: 
7: function getKey(): Buffer {
8:   const key = process.env.FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY;
9:   if (!key) {
10:     throw new Error('FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY environment variable is not set');
11:   }
12:   // Key must be 32 bytes for AES-256. Hash it to ensure correct length.
13:   return crypto.createHash('sha256').update(key).digest();
14: }
15: 
16: export function encrypt(plaintext: string): string {
17:   const key = getKey();
18:   const iv = crypto.randomBytes(IV_LENGTH);
19:   const cipher = crypto.createCipheriv(ALGORITHM, key, iv);
20: 
21:   let encrypted = cipher.update(plaintext, 'utf8', 'hex');
22:   encrypted += cipher.final('hex');
23:   const authTag = cipher.getAuthTag();
24: 
25:   // Store as iv:authTag:ciphertext
26:   return `${iv.toString('hex')}:${authTag.toString('hex')}:${encrypted}`;
27: }
28: 
29: export function decrypt(ciphertext: string): string {
30:   const key = getKey();
31:   const parts = ciphertext.split(':');
32:   if (parts.length !== 3) {
33:     throw new Error('Invalid encrypted value format');
34:   }
35: 
36:   const iv = Buffer.from(parts[0], 'hex');
37:   const authTag = Buffer.from(parts[1], 'hex');
38:   const encrypted = parts[2];
39: 
40:   const decipher = crypto.createDecipheriv(ALGORITHM, key, iv);
41:   decipher.setAuthTag(authTag);
42: 
43:   let decrypted = decipher.update(encrypted, 'hex', 'utf8');
44:   decrypted += decipher.final('utf8');
45:   return decrypted;
46: }
47: 
48: export const encryptedColumnTransformer = {
49:   to: (value: string | null): string | null => {
50:     if (value === null || value === undefined || value === '') return value;
51:     return encrypt(value);
52:   },
53:   from: (value: string | null): string | null => {
54:     if (value === null || value === undefined || value === '') return value;
55:     try {
56:       return decrypt(value);
57:     } catch {
58:       return value;
59:     }
60:   },
61: };

4: 
5: @Injectable()
6: export class JwtStrategy extends PassportStrategy(Strategy) {
7:   constructor() {
8:     const secret = process.env.JWT_SECRET;
9:     if (!secret) {

2:   Controller,
3:   Post,
4:   Body,
5:   Req,
6:   Inject,
7:   HttpCode,
8:   HttpStatus,
9:   BadRequestException,
10:   UnauthorizedException,
11:   ServiceUnavailableException,
12: } from '@nestjs/common';
13: import { InjectRepository } from '@nestjs/typeorm';
14: import { Repository } from 'typeorm';
15: import { Request } from 'express';
16: import { Public } from '../common/decorators/public.decorator';
17: import { IPaymentConnector, PAYMENT_CONNECTOR } from './interfaces/payment-connector.interface';
18: import { PaymentIdempotencyKey } from './entities/payment-idempotency-key.entity';
19: import { PaymentCallbackDto } from './dto/payment-callback.dto';
20: 
21: @Controller('payments')
22: export class PaymentCallbackController {
23:   constructor(
24:     @Inject(PAYMENT_CONNECTOR)
25:     private readonly connector: IPaymentConnector,
26:     @InjectRepository(PaymentIdempotencyKey)
27:     private readonly idempotencyRepo: Repository<PaymentIdempotencyKey>,
28:   ) {}
29: 
30:   /**
31:    * Receives inbound payment-provider webhooks / callbacks.
32:    *
33:    * - Disabled by default: requires PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true to process callbacks.
34:    *   When disabled, returns 503 to prevent silent noop acceptance in offline/local mode.
35:    * - Marked @Public() ΓÇö callbacks arrive without a user JWT.
36:    * - Signature is verified via `connector.verifyCallback()`; the noop connector
37:    *   always returns true so no external dependency is needed locally.
38:    * - Idempotent: a duplicate callback with the same key returns the stored result
39:    *   without any side effects.
40:    * - The idempotency key is resolved from (in order of preference):
41:    *     1. `body.idempotencyKey`
42:    *     2. `X-Idempotency-Key` request header
43:    */
44:   @Public()
45:   @Post('callback')
46:   @HttpCode(HttpStatus.OK)
47:   async handleCallback(
48:     @Body() body: PaymentCallbackDto,
49:     @Req() req: Request,
50:   ): Promise<{
51:     processed: boolean;
52:     alreadyProcessed: boolean;
53:     result: Record<string, unknown>;
54:   }> {
55:     // Payment callbacks are disabled by default. An explicit opt-in is required
56:     // to prevent the permissive noop connector from silently acting as production
57:     // acceptance in offline/local deployments.
58:     if (process.env.PAYMENTS_ENABLED !== 'true') {
59:       throw new ServiceUnavailableException(
60:         'Payment callbacks are disabled. Set PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true to enable.',
61:       );
62:     }
63: 
64:     // Verify provider signature ΓÇö noop always passes; real connectors validate HMAC/JWT
65:     const headers = req.headers as Record<string, string>;
66:     if (!this.connector.verifyCallback(headers, body)) {
67:       throw new UnauthorizedException('Invalid callback signature');
68:     }
69: 
70:     // Resolve idempotency key
71:     const key =
72:       body.idempotencyKey?.trim() || (headers['x-idempotency-key'] ?? '').trim();
73:     if (!key) {
74:       throw new BadRequestException(
75:         'Missing idempotency key ΓÇö supply body.idempotencyKey or X-Idempotency-Key header',
76:       );
77:     }
78: 
79:     // Idempotency check: return cached result for duplicate deliveries
80:     const existing = await this.idempotencyRepo.findOne({ where: { key } });
81:     if (existing) {
82:       return {
83:         processed: true,
84:         alreadyProcessed: true,
85:         result: existing.result as Record<string, unknown>,
86:       };
87:     }
88: 
89:     // Process the callback (connector-specific logic would go here for real providers)
90:     const result: Record<string, unknown> = {
91:       success: true,
92:       event: body.event ?? 'unknown',
93:       connectorName: body.connectorName ?? this.connector.name,
94:       processedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
95:     };
96: 
97:     await this.idempotencyRepo.save({
98:       key,
99:       connectorName: body.connectorName ?? this.connector.name,
100:       operation: `CALLBACK:${body.event ?? 'unknown'}`,
101:       result,
102:     });
103: 
104:     return { processed: true, alreadyProcessed: false, result };
105:   }
106: }

9: // ΓöÇΓöÇ Service worker cache helpers ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
10: 
11: function swPostMessage(msg: Record<string, unknown>): void {
12:   if ('serviceWorker' in navigator && navigator.serviceWorker.controller) {
13:     navigator.serviceWorker.controller.postMessage(msg);
14:   }
15: }
16: 
17: function swClearCache(): Promise<void> {
18:   return new Promise((resolve) => {
19:     if (!('serviceWorker' in navigator) || !navigator.serviceWorker.controller) {
20:       resolve();
21:       return;
22:     }
23:     const channel = new MessageChannel();
24:     channel.port1.onmessage = () => resolve();
25:     navigator.serviceWorker.controller.postMessage({ type: 'CLEAR_CACHE' }, [channel.port2]);
26:     setTimeout(resolve, 300);
27:   });
28: }
29: 
30: // ΓöÇΓöÇ Context ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
31: 
32: export interface AuthUserExtended extends AuthUser {
33:   mustChangePassword?: boolean;
34: }
35: 
36: interface AuthContextType {
37:   user: AuthUserExtended | null;
38:   loading: boolean;
39:   error: string | null;
40:   login: (username: string, password: string) => Promise<AuthUserExtended>;
41:   logout: () => Promise<void>;
42:   clearError: () => void;
43: }
44: 
45: const AuthContext = createContext<AuthContextType | null>(null);
46: 
47: export function AuthProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
48:   const [user, setUser] = useState<AuthUserExtended | null>(null);
49:   const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
50:   const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
51: 
52:   // Try to restore session on mount
53:   useEffect(() => {
54:     fetch('/api/auth/refresh', { method: 'POST', credentials: 'include' })
55:       .then((r) => (r.ok ? r.json() : null))
56:       .then((data: { accessToken: string; user: AuthUserExtended } | null) => {
57:         if (data) {
58:           setAccessToken(data.accessToken);
59:           setUser(data.user);
60:           swPostMessage({ type: 'SET_USER', userId: data.user.id });
61:         }
62:       })
63:       .catch(() => setUser(null))
64:       .finally(() => setLoading(false));
65:   }, []);
66: 
67:   const login = useCallback(async (username: string, password: string): Promise<AuthUserExtended> => {
68:     setError(null);
69:     try {
70:       const result = await apiLogin(username, password);
71:       const userData = result.user as AuthUserExtended;
72:       setUser(userData);
73:       swPostMessage({ type: 'SET_USER', userId: userData.id });
74:       return userData;
75:     } catch (err) {
76:       const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Login failed';
77:       setError(message);
78:       throw err;
79:     }
80:   }, []);
81: 
82:   const logout = useCallback(async () => {
83:     // Wipe SW cache before clearing server session so stale protected data is gone
84:     await swClearCache();
85:     await apiLogout();
86:     setUser(null);
87:   }, []);
88: 
89:   const clearError = useCallback(() => setError(null), []);
90: 
91:   return (
92:     <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, loading, error, login, logout, clearError }}>
93:       {children}
94:     </AuthContext.Provider>
95:   );
96: }
97: 
98: export function useAuth(): AuthContextType {
99:   const ctx = useContext(AuthContext);
100:   if (!ctx) throw new Error('useAuth must be used within AuthProvider');
101:   return ctx;
102: }

<type>file</type>
<content>1: /**
2:  * GreenLeaf Service Worker ΓÇö offline Knowledge Base cache
3:  *
4:  * Security: caches are scoped per user ID.  On logout all caches are wiped so
5:  * a subsequent user on the same workstation cannot access stale protected data.
6:  */
7: 
8: const CACHE_VERSION = 'v2';
9: const ARTICLE_API_PATTERN = /\/api\/(articles|plant-care)/;
10: 
11: let activeUserId = null;
12: 
13: function userCacheName() {
14:   return activeUserId ? `greenleaf-kb-${CACHE_VERSION}-${activeUserId}` : null;
15: }
16: 
17: async function networkFirst(request) {
18:   const cacheName = userCacheName();
19:   if (!cacheName) {
20:     // No authenticated user ΓÇö never serve from cache
21:     return fetch(request);
22:   }
23: 
24:   const cache = await caches.open(cacheName);
25:   try {
26:     const response = await fetch(request);
27:     if (response.ok) {
28:       cache.put(request, response.clone());
29:     }
30:     return response;
31:   } catch {
32:     const cached = await cache.match(request);
33:     if (cached) return cached;
34:     return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Offline and no cached data available' }), {
35:       status: 503,
36:       headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
37:     });
38:   }
39: }
40: 
41: async function clearAllCaches() {
42:   const keys = await caches.keys();
43:   await Promise.all(keys.map((k) => caches.delete(k)));
44:   activeUserId = null;
45: }
46: 
47: // ΓöÇΓöÇ Message handling ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
48: 
49: self.addEventListener('message', (event) => {
50:   const { type, userId } = event.data || {};
51: 
52:   if (type === 'SET_USER') {
53:     // Called after login ΓÇö scope cache to this user
54:     activeUserId = userId || null;
55:   }
56: 
57:   if (type === 'CLEAR_CACHE') {
58:     // Called on logout ΓÇö wipe everything so the next user starts clean
59:     clearAllCaches().catch(() => {});
60:     if (event.ports && event.ports[0]) {
61:       event.ports[0].postMessage({ cleared: true });
62:     }
63:   }
64: });
65: 
66: // ΓöÇΓöÇ Fetch interception ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
67: 
68: self.addEventListener('fetch', (event) => {
69:   if (ARTICLE_API_PATTERN.test(event.request.url)) {
70:     event.respondWith(networkFirst(event.request));
71:   }
72: });
73: 
74: // ΓöÇΓöÇ Activation: remove stale (version-bumped) caches ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
75: 
76: self.addEventListener('activate', (event) => {
77:   event.waitUntil(
78:     caches.keys().then((keys) =>
79:       Promise.all(
80:         keys
81:           .filter((k) => !k.startsWith(`greenleaf-kb-${CACHE_VERSION}-`))
82:           .map((k) => caches.delete(k)),
83:       ),
84:     ),
85:   );
86: });

(End of file - total 86 lines)
</content>

<content>1: const API_BASE = '/api';
2: 
3: export interface AuthUser {
4:   id: string;
5:   username: string;
6:   role: string;
7: }
8: 
9: export interface LoginResponse {
10:   accessToken: string;
11:   user: AuthUser;
12: }
13: 
14: let accessToken: string | null = null;
15: 
16: export function getAccessToken(): string | null {
17:   return accessToken;
18: }
19: 
20: export function setAccessToken(token: string | null): void {
21:   accessToken = token;
22: }
23: 
24: export async function apiFetch(path: string, options: RequestInit = {}): Promise<Response> {
25:   const headers: Record<string, string> = {
26:     'Content-Type': 'application/json',
27:     ...(options.headers as Record<string, string>),
28:   };
29: 
30:   if (accessToken) {
31:     headers['Authorization'] = `Bearer ${accessToken}`;
32:   }
33: 
34:   const res = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, {
35:     ...options,
36:     headers,
37:     credentials: 'include',
38:   });
39: 
40:   if (res.status === 401 && accessToken) {
41:     // Try to refresh
42:     const refreshed = await refreshToken();
43:     if (refreshed) {
44:       headers['Authorization'] = `Bearer ${accessToken}`;
45:       return fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, {
46:         ...options,
47:         headers,
48:         credentials: 'include',
49:       });
50:     }
51:   }
52: 
53:   return res;
54: }
55: 
56: export async function login(username: string, password: string): Promise<LoginResponse> {
57:   const res = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/auth/login`, {
58:     method: 'POST',
59:     headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
60:     credentials: 'include',
61:     body: JSON.stringify({ username, password }),
62:   });
63: 
64:   if (!res.ok) {
65:     const body = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));
66:     throw new Error(body.message || 'Login failed');
67:   }
68: 
69:   const data: LoginResponse = await res.json();
70:   accessToken = data.accessToken;
71:   return data;
72: }
73: 
74: export async function refreshToken(): Promise<boolean> {
75:   try {
76:     const res = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/auth/refresh`, {
77:       method: 'POST',
78:       credentials: 'include',
79:     });
80: 
81:     if (!res.ok) {
82:       accessToken = null;
83:       return false;
84:     }
85: 
86:     const data = await res.json();
87:     accessToken = data.accessToken;
88:     return true;
89:   } catch {
90:     accessToken = null;
91:     return false;
92:   }
93: }
94: 
95: export async function logout(): Promise<void> {
96:   await fetch(`${API_BASE}/auth/logout`, {
97:     method: 'POST',
98:     credentials: 'include',
99:   }).catch(() => {});
100:   accessToken = null;
101: }

(End of file - total 101 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { Injectable, NotFoundException } from '@nestjs/common';
2: import { InjectRepository } from '@nestjs/typeorm';
3: import { Repository, ILike, FindOptionsWhere } from 'typeorm';
4: import { CatalogItem } from './entities/catalog-item.entity';
5: import { CreateCatalogItemDto } from './dto/create-catalog-item.dto';
6: import { UpdateCatalogItemDto } from './dto/update-catalog-item.dto';
7: import { QueryCatalogItemsDto } from './dto/query-catalog-items.dto';
8: import { DataQualityService } from '../data-quality/data-quality.service';
9: 
10: @Injectable()
11: export class CatalogService {
12:   constructor(
13:     @InjectRepository(CatalogItem)
14:     private readonly catalogRepo: Repository<CatalogItem>,
15:     private readonly dataQuality: DataQualityService,
16:   ) {}
17: 
18:   private buildFingerprint(item: { title: string; supplierId?: string | null; unitSize?: string | null; upc?: string | null }): string {
19:     return this.dataQuality.generateFingerprint([
20:       item.title,
21:       item.supplierId ?? undefined,
22:       item.unitSize ?? undefined,
23:       item.upc ?? undefined,
24:     ]);
25:   }
26: 
27:   async findAll(query: QueryCatalogItemsDto) {
28:     const page = parseInt(query.page || '1', 10);
29:     const limit = Math.min(parseInt(query.limit || '20', 10), 100);
30:     const skip = (page - 1) * limit;
31: 
32:     const where: FindOptionsWhere<CatalogItem>[] = [];
33:     const baseWhere: FindOptionsWhere<CatalogItem> = {};
34: 
35:     if (query.supplierId) baseWhere.supplierId = query.supplierId;
36:     if (query.isActive !== undefined) baseWhere.isActive = query.isActive === 'true';
37: 
38:     if (query.search) {
39:       where.push(
40:         { ...baseWhere, title: ILike(`%${query.search}%`) },
41:         { ...baseWhere, description: ILike(`%${query.search}%`) },
42:       );
43:     } else {
44:       where.push(baseWhere);
45:     }
46: 
47:     const sortBy = query.sortBy || 'createdAt';
48:     const sortOrder = (query.sortOrder?.toUpperCase() === 'ASC' ? 'ASC' : 'DESC') as 'ASC' | 'DESC';
49:     const allowedSortFields = ['title', 'unitPrice', 'isActive', 'createdAt'];
50:     const orderField = allowedSortFields.includes(sortBy) ? sortBy : 'createdAt';
51: 
52:     const [data, total] = await this.catalogRepo.findAndCount({
53:       where,
54:       relations: ['supplier'],
55:       order: { [orderField]: sortOrder },
56:       skip,
57:       take: limit,
58:     });
59: 
60:     return {
61:       data,
62:       meta: { page, limit, total, totalPages: Math.ceil(total / limit) },
63:     };
64:   }
65: 
66:   async findById(id: string): Promise<CatalogItem> {
67:     const item = await this.catalogRepo.findOne({
68:       where: { id },
69:       relations: ['supplier'],
70:     });
71:     if (!item) throw new NotFoundException('Catalog item not found');
72:     return item;
73:   }
74: 
75:   async create(dto: CreateCatalogItemDto): Promise<CatalogItem> {
76:     const fingerprint = this.buildFingerprint(dto as any);
77:     const item = this.catalogRepo.create({
78:       ...dto,
79:       fingerprint,
80:     });
81:     const saved = await this.catalogRepo.save(item);
82:     this.dataQuality
83:       .checkForDuplicates('CatalogItem', saved.id, fingerprint)
84:       .catch(() => undefined);
85:     return this.findById(saved.id);
86:   }
87: 
88:   async update(id: string, dto: UpdateCatalogItemDto): Promise<CatalogItem> {
89:     const item = await this.findById(id);
90:     Object.assign(item, dto);
91:     const fingerprint = this.buildFingerprint(item);
92:     item.fingerprint = fingerprint;
93:     const saved = await this.catalogRepo.save(item);
94:     this.dataQuality
95:       .checkForDuplicates('CatalogItem', saved.id, fingerprint)
96:       .catch(() => undefined);
97:     return this.findById(saved.id);
98:   }
99: 
100:   async findAllForDropdown(): Promise<Pick<CatalogItem, 'id' | 'title'>[]> {
101:     return this.catalogRepo.find({
102:       where: { isActive: true },
103:       select: ['id', 'title'],
104:       order: { title: 'ASC' },
105:     });
106:   }
107: }

(End of file - total 107 lines)

<path>C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\Claude task\Task-1\repo\server\src\observability\scheduler.service.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { Injectable, Logger, OnModuleInit } from '@nestjs/common';
2: import { Cron, CronExpression } from '@nestjs/schedule';
3: import { DataSource } from 'typeorm';
4: import { ObservabilityService } from './observability.service';
5: import { DataQualityService } from '../data-quality/data-quality.service';
6: import { NotificationService } from '../notifications/notification.service';
7: import { NotificationType } from '../common/enums/notification-type.enum';
8: import { withRetry } from '../common/helpers/retry.helper';
9: 
10: @Injectable()
11: export class SchedulerService implements OnModuleInit {
12:   private readonly logger = new Logger(SchedulerService.name);
13: 
14:   constructor(
15:     private readonly observability: ObservabilityService,
16:     private readonly dataQuality: DataQualityService,
17:     private readonly notificationService: NotificationService,
18:     private readonly dataSource: DataSource,
19:   ) {}
20: 
21:   onModuleInit() {
22:     this.logger.log('Scheduler service initialized');
23:   }
24: 
25:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Core job runner ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
26: 
27:   async runJob(jobName: string, fn: () => Promise<void>): Promise<void> {
28:     this.logger.log(`Starting job: ${jobName}`);
29:     const start = Date.now();
30:     let runId: string | null = null;
31: 
32:     try {
33:       const { attempts } = await withRetry(async (attempt) => {
34:         if (attempt > 1) {
35:           this.logger.warn(`Retrying job ${jobName}, attempt ${attempt}`);
36:         }
37:         runId = (await this.observability.startJobRun(jobName, attempt)).id;
38:         await fn();
39:       }, 3);
40: 
41:       const durationMs = Date.now() - start;
42:       if (runId) {
43:         await this.observability.completeJobRun(runId, durationMs);
44:       }
45:       this.logger.log(`Job ${jobName} completed in ${durationMs}ms (attempts: ${attempts})`);
46: 
47:       await this.observability.writeLog({
48:         level: 'INFO',
49:         service: 'Scheduler',
50:         message: `Job ${jobName} succeeded in ${durationMs}ms`,
51:       });
52:     } catch (err) {
53:       const durationMs = Date.now() - start;
54:       const errorMessage = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
55: 
56:       if (runId) {
57:         await this.observability.failJobRun(runId, durationMs, errorMessage);
58:       }
59: 
60:       this.logger.error(`Job ${jobName} failed after all retries: ${errorMessage}`);
61: 
62:       await this.observability.writeLog({
63:         level: 'ERROR',
64:         service: 'Scheduler',
65:         message: `Job ${jobName} FAILED after all retries: ${errorMessage}`,
66:       });
67: 
68:       // Notify all administrators
69:       await this.notifyAdmins(jobName, errorMessage);
70:     }
71:   }
72: 
73:   private async notifyAdmins(jobName: string, errorMessage: string): Promise<void> {
74:     try {
75:       const admins = await this.dataSource.query<Array<{ id: string }>>(
76:         `SELECT id FROM users WHERE role = 'ADMINISTRATOR' AND "isActive" = true`,
77:       );
78:       for (const admin of admins) {
79:         await this.notificationService.emit(
80:           admin.id,
81:           NotificationType.SYSTEM_ALERT,
82:           `Background job failed: ${jobName}`,
83:           `The scheduled job "${jobName}" failed after 3 attempts. Error: ${errorMessage}`,
84:         );
85:       }
86:     } catch (e) {
87:       this.logger.error(`Failed to notify admins about job failure: ${e}`);
88:     }
89:   }
90: 
91:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Manually trigger a job by name (used by retry endpoint) ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
92: 
93:   async triggerJob(jobName: string): Promise<void> {
94:     switch (jobName) {
95:       case 'dedup-scan':
96:         return this.runJob('dedup-scan', () => this.dataQuality.runDedupScan());
97:       case 'data-quality-check':
98:         return this.runJob('data-quality-check', () =>
99:           this.dataQuality.runQualityChecks().then(() => undefined),
100:         );
101:       case 'notification-queue-drain':
102:         return this.runJob('notification-queue-drain', () =>
103:           this.dataSource.transaction((manager) =>
104:             this.notificationService.drainQueue(manager),
105:           ),
106:         );
107:       case 'session-cleanup':
108:         return this.runJob('session-cleanup', () => this.cleanExpiredSessions());
109:       default:
110:         throw new Error(`Unknown job: ${jobName}`);
111:     }
112:   }
113: 
114:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Scheduled jobs ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
115: 
116:   @Cron(CronExpression.EVERY_6_HOURS)
117:   async scheduledDedupScan(): Promise<void> {
118:     await this.runJob('dedup-scan', () => this.dataQuality.runDedupScan());
119:   }
120: 
121:   @Cron(CronExpression.EVERY_6_HOURS)
122:   async scheduledDataQualityCheck(): Promise<void> {
123:     await this.runJob('data-quality-check', () =>
124:       this.dataQuality.runQualityChecks().then(() => undefined),
125:     );
126:   }
127: 
128:   @Cron(CronExpression.EVERY_HOUR)
129:   async scheduledQueueDrain(): Promise<void> {
130:     await this.runJob('notification-queue-drain', () =>
131:       this.dataSource.transaction((manager) =>
132:         this.notificationService.drainQueue(manager),
133:       ),
134:     );
135:   }
136: 
137:   @Cron(CronExpression.EVERY_HOUR)
138:   async scheduledSessionCleanup(): Promise<void> {
139:     await this.runJob('session-cleanup', () => this.cleanExpiredSessions());
140:   }
141: 
142:   private async cleanExpiredSessions(): Promise<void> {
143:     await this.dataSource.query(`DELETE FROM sessions WHERE "expiresAt" < now()`);
144:   }
145: }

(End of file - total 145 lines)
</content>

<path>C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\Claude task\Task-1\repo\server\src\common\helpers\retry.helper.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: const BASE_DELAY_MS = 1000;
2: const BACKOFF_FACTOR = 4;
3: 
4: /**
5:  * Returns delay in ms for a given attempt number (1-indexed).
6:  * Attempt 1 ΓåÆ 1 000 ms
7:  * Attempt 2 ΓåÆ 4 000 ms
8:  * Attempt 3 ΓåÆ 16 000 ms
9:  */
10: export function exponentialDelay(attempt: number): number {
11:   return BASE_DELAY_MS * Math.pow(BACKOFF_FACTOR, attempt - 1);
12: }
13: 
14: export async function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
15:   return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
16: }
17: 
18: /**
19:  * Executes fn with up to maxAttempts retries using exponential backoff.
20:  * Throws the last error if all attempts fail.
21:  */
22: export async function withRetry<T>(
23:   fn: (attempt: number) => Promise<T>,
24:   maxAttempts = 3,
25: ): Promise<{ result: T; attempts: number }> {
26:   let lastError: Error = new Error('Unknown error');
27:   for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++) {
28:     try {
29:       const result = await fn(attempt);
30:       return { result, attempts: attempt };
31:     } catch (err) {
32:       lastError = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err));
33:       if (attempt < maxAttempts) {
34:         await sleep(exponentialDelay(attempt));
35:       }
36:     }
37:   }
38:   throw lastError;
39: }

(End of file - total 39 lines)
</content>

20: 21: interface LineState { 22: poLineItem: PoLineItem; 23: quantityExpected: number; 24: quantityReceived: string; 25: varianceReasonCode: string; 26: varianceNotes: string; 27: putawayLocationId: string; 44: const [lines, setLines] = useState([]); 45: const [notes, setNotes] = useState(''); 46: const [entryMode, setEntryMode] = useState('MANUAL'); 47: const [scanInput, setScanInput] = useState(''); 48: const [lastScanFeedback, setLastScanFeedback] = useState(null); 49: const [submitting, setSubmitting] = useState(false); 50: const [error, setError] = useState(null); 51: const scanInputRef = useRef(null); 52: const manualInputRefs = useRef<(HTMLInputElement | null)[]>([]); 53: 54: useEffect(() => { 55: Promise.all([ 56: fetchPos({ status: 'ISSUED', limit: '100' }), 57: fetchPos({ status: 'PARTIALLY_RECEIVED', limit: '100' }), 58: fetchPutawayLocations(), 59: ]).then(([issued, partial, locs]) => { 60: // Deduplicate by PO id ΓÇö a PO may appear in both ISSUED and 61: // PARTIALLY_RECEIVED results if its status changed between requests. 62: const combined = [...issued.data, ...partial.data]; 63: const seen = new Set(); 64: const unique = combined.filter((po) => { 65: if (seen.has(po.id)) return false; 66: seen.add(po.id); 67: return true; 68: }); 69: setPos(unique); 70: setLocations(locs); 71: }).catch((err) => setError(err.message)); 72: }, []); 73: 74: // Auto-focus scan input whenever barcode mode is active and a PO is selected 75: useEffect(() => { 76: if (entryMode === 'BARCODE' && selectedPoId && scanInputRef.current) { 77: scanInputRef.current.focus(); 78: } 79: }, [entryMode, selectedPoId]); 80: 81: const handlePoSelect = (poId: string) => { 82: setSelectedPoId(poId); 83: setLastScanFeedback(null); 84: const po = pos.find((p) => p.id === poId); 85: if (!po) { setLines([]); return; } 86: setLines( 87: po.lineItems.map((li) => ({ 88: poLineItem: li, 89: quantityExpected: Math.max(0, Number(li.quantity) - Number(li.quantityReceived)), 90: quantityReceived: String(Math.max(0, Number(li.quantity) - Number(li.quantityReceived))), 91: varianceReasonCode: 'NONE', 92: varianceNotes: '', 93: putawayLocationId: '', 94: })), 95: ); 96: }; 97: 98: const updateLine = (index: number, field: keyof LineState, value: string) => { 99: setLines((prev) => 100: prev.map((l, i) => (i === index ? { ...l, [field]: value } : l)), 101: ); 102: }; 103: 104: const handleManualQtyKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent, index: number) => { 105: if (e.key === 'Enter') { 106: e.preventDefault(); 107: const next = manualInputRefs.current[index + 1]; 108: if (next) next.focus(); 109: } 110: }; 111: 112: /** 113: * Barcode scan handler. 114: * Matches the scanned code against each line's scan code (catalogItemId or UUID prefix). 115: * On match, increments quantityReceived by 1. 116: */ 117: const handleScan = useCallback(() => { 118: const code = scanInput.trim(); 119: if (!code) return; 120: 121: const idx = lines.findIndex( 122: (l) => getScanCode(l.poLineItem).toUpperCase() === code.toUpperCase(), 123: ); 124: 125: if (idx === -1) { 126: setLastScanFeedback(`No match for code "${code}"`); 133: setLastScanFeedback( 134: `Scanned: ${line.poLineItem.description} ΓÇö Qty now ${newQty}`, 135: ); 136: } 137: 138: setScanInput(''); 139: // Re-focus for next scan 140: scanInputRef.current?.focus(); 141: }, [scanInput, lines]); 142: 143: const handleScanKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => { 144: if (e.key === 'Enter') { 145: e.preventDefault(); 146: handleScan(); 147: } 148: }; 149: 150: const variance = (line: LineState) => 151: Number(line.quantityReceived) - line.quantityExpected; 161: return; 162: } 163: } 164: 165: setSubmitting(true); 166: try { 167: const receipt = await createReceipt({ 168: poId: selectedPoId, 169: entryMode, 170: notes: notes || undefined, 171: lineItems: lines.map((l) => ({ 172: poLineItemId: l.poLineItem.id, 173: quantityReceived: Number(l.quantityReceived), 174: varianceReasonCode: l.varianceReasonCode !== 'NONE' ? l.varianceReasonCode : undefined, 175: varianceNotes: l.varianceNotes || undefined, 176: putawayLocationId: l.putawayLocationId || undefined, 177: })), 178: }); <content>1: import { Injectable, NotFoundException } from '@nestjs/common';
2: import { InjectRepository } from '@nestjs/typeorm';
3: import { Repository, DataSource, EntityManager } from 'typeorm';
4: import { FundsLedgerEntry } from './entities/funds-ledger-entry.entity';
5: import { LedgerEntryType } from '../common/enums/ledger-entry-type.enum';
6: import { CreateDepositDto } from './dto/create-deposit.dto';
7: import { CreateAdjustmentDto } from './dto/create-adjustment.dto';
8: import { QueryLedgerDto } from './dto/query-ledger.dto';
9: 
10: @Injectable()
11: export class FundsLedgerService {
12:   constructor(
13:     @InjectRepository(FundsLedgerEntry)
14:     private readonly entryRepo: Repository<FundsLedgerEntry>,
15:     private readonly dataSource: DataSource,
16:   ) {}
17: 
18:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Internal entry creator (must run inside a transaction) ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
19: 
20:   private async createEntry(
21:     manager: EntityManager,
22:     supplierId: string,
23:     type: LedgerEntryType,
24:     amount: number,
25:     referenceType: string | null,
26:     referenceId: string | null,
27:     description: string | null,
28:     createdBy: string | null,
29:   ): Promise<FundsLedgerEntry> {
30:     // Advisory lock ensures single-writer serialization per supplier
31:     await manager.query(`SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext($1))`, [supplierId]);
32: 
33:     // Get current running balance from the most recent entry
34:     const result = await manager.query(
35:       `SELECT "runningBalance" FROM funds_ledger_entries
36:        WHERE "supplierId" = $1
37:        ORDER BY "createdAt" DESC
38:        LIMIT 1`,
39:       [supplierId],
40:     );
41:     const currentBalance = result.length > 0 ? Number(result[0].runningBalance) : 0;
42:     const newBalance = parseFloat((currentBalance + amount).toFixed(2));
43: 
44:     const entry = manager.create(FundsLedgerEntry, {
45:       supplierId,
46:       type,
47:       amount,
48:       runningBalance: newBalance,
49:       referenceType,
50:       referenceId: referenceId ?? null,
51:       description,
52:       createdBy,
53:     });
54:     return manager.save(FundsLedgerEntry, entry);
55:   }
56: 
57:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Public mutation methods ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
58: 
59:   async recordDeposit(
60:     supplierId: string,
61:     amount: number,
62:     referenceType?: string,
63:     referenceId?: string,
64:     description?: string,
65:     createdBy?: string,
66:   ): Promise<void> {
67:     await this.dataSource.transaction(async (manager) => {
68:       await this.createEntry(
69:         manager,
70:         supplierId,
71:         LedgerEntryType.DEPOSIT,
72:         Math.abs(amount),
73:         referenceType ?? null,
74:         referenceId ?? null,
75:         description ?? null,
76:         createdBy ?? null,
77:       );
78:     });
79:   }
80: 
81:   async recordEscrowHold(
82:     supplierId: string,
83:     amount: number,
84:     poId: string,
85:     createdBy?: string,
86:   ): Promise<void> {
87:     await this.dataSource.transaction(async (manager) => {
88:       await this.createEntry(
89:         manager,
90:         supplierId,
91:         LedgerEntryType.ESCROW_HOLD,
92:         -Math.abs(amount),
93:         'PURCHASE_ORDER',
94:         poId,
95:         `Escrow hold for PO`,
96:         createdBy ?? null,
97:       );
98:     });
99:   }
100: 
101:   async releaseEscrow(
102:     supplierId: string,
103:     amount: number,
104:     poId: string,
105:     createdBy?: string,
106:   ): Promise<void> {
107:     await this.dataSource.transaction(async (manager) => {
108:       await this.createEntry(
109:         manager,
110:         supplierId,
111:         LedgerEntryType.ESCROW_RELEASE,
112:         Math.abs(amount),
113:         'PURCHASE_ORDER',
114:         poId,
115:         `Escrow release for PO`,
116:         createdBy ?? null,
117:       );
118:     });
119:   }
120: 
121:   async recordPayment(
122:     supplierId: string,
123:     amount: number,
124:     poId: string,
125:     createdBy?: string,
126:   ): Promise<void> {
127:     await this.dataSource.transaction(async (manager) => {
128:       await this.createEntry(
129:         manager,
130:         supplierId,
131:         LedgerEntryType.PAYMENT,
132:         -Math.abs(amount),
133:         'PURCHASE_ORDER',
134:         poId,
135:         `Payment for PO`,
136:         createdBy ?? null,
137:       );
138:     });
139:   }
140: 
141:   async recordRefund(
142:     supplierId: string,
143:     amount: number,
144:     raId: string,
145:     createdBy?: string,
146:   ): Promise<void> {
147:     if (!supplierId) return; // no-op if supplier unknown
148:     await this.dataSource.transaction(async (manager) => {
149:       await this.createEntry(
150:         manager,
151:         supplierId,
152:         LedgerEntryType.REFUND,
153:         Math.abs(amount),
154:         'RETURN_AUTHORIZATION',
155:         raId,
156:         `Refund for return authorization`,
157:         createdBy ?? null,
158:       );
159:     });
160:   }
161: 
162:   async recordAdjustment(
163:     supplierId: string,
164:     amount: number,
165:     description: string,
166:     referenceType?: string,
167:     referenceId?: string,
168:     createdBy?: string,
169:   ): Promise<void> {
170:     await this.dataSource.transaction(async (manager) => {
171:       await this.createEntry(
172:         manager,
173:         supplierId,
174:         LedgerEntryType.ADJUSTMENT,
175:         amount,
176:         referenceType ?? null,
177:         referenceId ?? null,
178:         description,
179:         createdBy ?? null,
180:       );
181:     });
182:   }
183: 
184:   // ΓöÇΓöÇ Query methods ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
185: 
186:   async getLedger(supplierId: string, query: QueryLedgerDto) {
187:     const page = parseInt(query.page || '1', 10);
188:     const limit = Math.min(parseInt(query.limit || '20', 10), 100);
189:     const skip = (page - 1) * limit;
190: 
191:     const qb = this.entryRepo
192:       .createQueryBuilder('e')
193:       .leftJoinAndSelect('e.creator', 'creator')
194:       .where('e.supplierId = :supplierId', { supplierId })
195:       .orderBy('e.createdAt', 'DESC')
196:       .skip(skip)
197:       .take(limit);
198: 
199:     if (query.dateFrom) {
200:       qb.andWhere('e.createdAt >= :from', { from: new Date(query.dateFrom) });
201:     }
202:     if (query.dateTo) {
203:       qb.andWhere('e.createdAt <= :to', { to: new Date(query.dateTo) });
204:     }
205: 
206:     const [data, total] = await qb.getManyAndCount();
207:     return { data, meta: { page, limit, total, totalPages: Math.ceil(total / limit) } };
208:   }
209: 
210:   async getLedgerSummary(supplierId: string) {
211:     const result = await this.entryRepo
212:       .createQueryBuilder('e')
213:       .select('e.type', 'type')
214:       .addSelect('SUM(e.amount)', 'total')
215:       .where('e.supplierId = :supplierId', { supplierId })
216:       .groupBy('e.type')
217:       .getRawMany<{ type: string; total: string }>();
218: 
219:     const byType = Object.fromEntries(result.map((r) => [r.type, Number(r.total)]));
220: 
221:     const totalDeposits = byType['DEPOSIT'] ?? 0;
222:     const totalPayments = Math.abs(byType['PAYMENT'] ?? 0);
223:     const totalEscrowHolds = Math.abs(byType['ESCROW_HOLD'] ?? 0);
224:     const totalRefunds = byType['REFUND'] ?? 0;
225: 
226:     // Current balance = latest runningBalance
227:     const latest = await this.entryRepo.findOne({
228:       where: { supplierId },
229:       order: { createdAt: 'DESC' },
230:       select: ['runningBalance'],
231:     });
232:     const currentBalance = latest ? Number(latest.runningBalance) : 0;
233: 
234:     return {
235:       totalDeposits,
236:       totalPayments,
237:       totalEscrowHolds,
238:       totalRefunds,
239:       currentBalance,
240:     };
241:   }
242: }

<content>1: import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';
2: 
3: /**
4:  * Procurement lifecycle E2E test ΓÇö API level.
5:  *
6:  * Tests the core business flow end-to-end:
7:  *   Purchase Request ΓåÆ Approval ΓåÆ PO (auto-generated) ΓåÆ Issue ΓåÆ Receipt ΓåÆ Return
8:  *
9:  * Uses Playwright's request fixture (HTTP API calls, no browser).
10:  * Runs against the E2E backend on port 3101 via Vite proxy on port 3100.
11:  *
12:  * Prerequisites (same as login.spec.ts):
13:  *   - Demo seed loaded (npm run seed:demo in repo/server)
14:  *   - Server compiled and running (Playwright starts it via webServer)
15:  *
16:  * Demo users used:
17:  *   - demo_admin  (ADMINISTRATOR) ΓÇö creates requests, issues POs, handles returns
18:  *   - demo_pm     (PROCUREMENT_MANAGER, isSupervisor=true) ΓÇö approves tier-1 requests
19:  *   - demo_clerk  (WAREHOUSE_CLERK) ΓÇö creates receipts
20:  */
21: 
22: const BASE = 'http://localhost:3101/api';
23: 
24: async function login(request: Parameters<typeof test>[1] extends { request: infer R } ? R : never, username: string, password: string): Promise<string> {
25:   const res = await request.post(`${BASE}/auth/login`, {
26:     data: { username, password },
27:   });
28:   expect(res.status()).toBe(200);
29:   const body = await res.json();
30:   return body.accessToken as string;
31: }
32: 
33: function authHeader(token: string) {
34:   return { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` };
35: }
36: 
37: test.describe('Procurement lifecycle', () => {
38:   test('happy path: request ΓåÆ supervisor approval ΓåÆ PO ΓåÆ receipt ΓåÆ return', async ({ request }) => {

import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';

/**
 * Procurement lifecycle E2E test ΓÇö API level.
 *
 * Tests the core business flow end-to-end:
 *   Purchase Request ΓåÆ Approval ΓåÆ PO (auto-generated) ΓåÆ Issue ΓåÆ Receipt ΓåÆ Return
 *
 * Uses Playwright's request fixture (HTTP API calls, no browser).
 * Runs against the E2E backend on port 3101 via Vite proxy on port 3100.
 *
 * Prerequisites (same as login.spec.ts):
 *   - Demo seed loaded (npm run seed:demo in repo/server)
 *   - Server compiled and running (Playwright starts it via webServer)
 *
 * Demo users used:
 *   - demo_admin  (ADMINISTRATOR) ΓÇö creates requests, issues POs, handles returns
 *   - demo_pm     (PROCUREMENT_MANAGER, isSupervisor=true) ΓÇö approves tier-1 requests
 *   - demo_clerk  (WAREHOUSE_CLERK) ΓÇö creates receipts
 */

<content>1: import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest';
2: 
3: /**
4:  * Tests for service worker cache isolation and logout invalidation.
5:  *
6:  * We test the SW logic directly by importing and exercising a module-level
7:  * recreation of the cache helpers, mirroring the behaviour in public/sw.js.
8:  */
9: 
10: // ΓöÇΓöÇ Re-implement the SW cache helpers in a testable module form ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
11: 
12: const CACHE_VERSION = 'v2';
13: 
14: function makeSwScope() {
15:   let activeUserId: string | null = null;
16:   const storedCaches: Record<string, Map<string, Response>> = {};
17: 
18:   const cacheApi = {
19:     open: vi.fn(async (name: string) => {
20:       if (!storedCaches[name]) storedCaches[name] = new Map();
21:       return {
22:         put: vi.fn((req: Request, res: Response) => storedCaches[name].set(req.url, res)),
23:         match: vi.fn(async (req: Request) => storedCaches[name].get(req.url) ?? undefined),
24:       };
25:     }),
26:     keys: vi.fn(async () => Object.keys(storedCaches)),
27:     delete: vi.fn(async (name: string) => { delete storedCaches[name]; return true; }),
28:   };
29: 
30:   function userCacheName() {
31:     return activeUserId ? `greenleaf-kb-${CACHE_VERSION}-${activeUserId}` : null;
32:   }
33: 
34:   async function networkFirst(request: Request, fetchImpl: typeof fetch) {
35:     const cacheName = userCacheName();
36:     if (!cacheName) return fetchImpl(request);
37:     const cache = await cacheApi.open(cacheName);
38:     try {
39:       const response = await fetchImpl(request);
40:       if ((response as Response).ok) cache.put(request, (response as Response).clone());
41:       return response;
42:     } catch {
43:       const cached = await cache.match(request);
44:       return (
45:         cached ??
46:         new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'offline' }), {
47:           status: 503,
48:           headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
49:         })
50:       );
51:     }
52:   }
53: 
54:   async function clearAllCaches() {
55:     const keys = await cacheApi.keys();
56:     await Promise.all(keys.map((k) => cacheApi.delete(k)));
57:     activeUserId = null;
58:   }
59: 
60:   function setUser(userId: string) { activeUserId = userId; }
61:   function getUser() { return activeUserId; }
62:   function getCaches() { return storedCaches; }
63: 
64:   return { networkFirst, clearAllCaches, setUser, getUser, getCaches, userCacheName };
65: }
66: 
67: // ΓöÇΓöÇ Tests ΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇΓöÇ
68: 
69: describe('Service worker cache isolation', () => {
70:   let sw: ReturnType<typeof makeSwScope>;
71:   let mockFetch: ReturnType<typeof vi.fn>;
72: 
73:   const makeReq = (url: string) => new Request(url);
74:   const makeOkRes = (body: string) =>
75:     new Response(body, { status: 200, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } });
76: 
77:   beforeEach(() => {
78:     sw = makeSwScope();
79:     mockFetch = vi.fn();
80:   });

<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { defineConfig, devices } from '@playwright/test';
2: 
3: /**
4:  * Playwright E2E configuration for the GreenLeaf Operations Suite.
5:  *
6:  * Launches two servers automatically:
7:  *   1. A dedicated NestJS backend on port 3101 with a relaxed login
8:  *      throttle (AUTH_LOGIN_THROTTLE_LIMIT=1000) so repeated test runs
9:  *      never hit 429.  Uses the same PostgreSQL database as the dev
10:  *      backend ΓÇö all demo-seed data is available.
11:  *   2. A Vite dev server on port 3100 whose proxy points at the E2E
12:  *      backend (VITE_API_PORT=3101).
13:  *
14:  * The developer's servers on ports 3000/3001 are not affected.
15:  *
16:  * Prerequisites:
17:  *   1. PostgreSQL running with greenleaf_db
18:  *   2. Demo seed loaded: cd repo/server && npm run seed:demo
19:  *   3. Server built:     cd repo/server && npx nest build
20:  *   4. Browsers:         cd repo && npx playwright install chromium
21:  *
22:  * Run:
23:  *   cd repo && npm run test:e2e
24:  */
25: export default defineConfig({
26:   testDir: './tests/e2e',
27:   fullyParallel: false,
28:   retries: 0,
29:   workers: 1,
30:   reporter: 'list',
31:   timeout: 30_000,
32: 
33:   webServer: [
